methods.description: Ask the backend to join a meeting via Recall.ai bot. Supports Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, and Webex. Emits bot:join over Socket.IO; the backend streams events back (bot:reply, bot:harness, bot:transcript, bot:left).

inputs.name: meet_url
inputs.comment: Meeting URL (Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, or Webex).

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.comment: Display name for the bot in the meeting. Defaults to OpenHuman.

inputs.name: platform
inputs.comment: Platform: gmeet, zoom, teams, or webex. Auto-detected from URL if omitted.

inputs.name: agent_name
inputs.comment: Optional AI agent display name forwarded to the backend bot.

inputs.name: system_prompt
inputs.comment: Optional custom meeting system prompt forwarded to the backend bot.

inputs.name: mascot_id
inputs.comment: Optional mascot ID selecting which Rive character appears in the meeting (e.g. "yellow").

inputs.name: rive_colors
inputs.comment: Optional Rive mascot color overrides forwarded to the backend bot.

inputs.name: mascots
inputs.comment: Optional dual-mascot config (issue #4277): array of up to 2 slots, each { mascotId, name?, riveColors?, voiceId? }. When present the backend renders both mascots and alternates the speaker per reply. `name` (from the manifest) enables name-addressed routing ("Hey Toshi …" → that slot). Absent falls back to mascot_id.

inputs.name: respond_to_participant
inputs.comment: Only respond to this participant's messages. Case-insensitive substring match against the speaker name in the transcript. Omit to respond to everyone.

inputs.name: wake_phrase
inputs.comment: Wake phrase the participant must say before the bot responds. When set, captions without this phrase are silently dropped. The phrase is stripped before the text reaches the LLM.

inputs.name: correlation_id
inputs.comment: Opaque correlation id echoed on all bot:* events for this session.

inputs.name: listen_only
inputs.comment: When true, the bot joins in listen-only mode (no microphone, no replies).

outputs.name: ok
outputs.comment: True when the join request was emitted.

outputs.name: platform
outputs.comment: Resolved platform: gmeet, zoom, teams, or webex.

Run the workflow_builder agent for one authoring turn. `mode` selects create (first draft from `instruction`), revise (refine the injected `graph`), repair (diagnose a failed `run_id` and fix), or build (instant-create: build + dry-run + propose against `flow_id`; propose-only, see #4596). The server renders the agent's brief — the frontend no longer crafts prompts. Returns `{ proposal, assistant_text, error }`, where `proposal` is the `{ type: 'workflow_proposal', name, graph, require_approval, summary, warnings }` the agent produced (or null). No mode auto-persists a graph; save/enable/run stay behind the user's explicit action.

inputs.name: instruction
inputs.comment: The user's ask: description (create/build) or change instruction (revise); optional note for repair.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.comment: The current draft WorkflowGraph, injected as context for revise/repair/build.

inputs.name: flow_id
inputs.comment: Saved flow id — required for `build` (save target); optional elsewhere (lets the agent run_flow it to test, with confirmation).

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.comment: Failed run id (== thread id) for `repair`, so the agent can get_flow_run it.

inputs.name: error
inputs.comment: Run-level error message for `repair`, if known.

inputs.name: failing_node_ids
inputs.comment: Node ids implicated in the failure, for `repair` (array of strings).

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.comment: Chat thread to stream this turn into (copilot/scout live view). Omit for a headless run — the blocking result is returned either way.

inputs.name: request_id

methods.description: Return the MINIMUM set of nodes covering all memory in a time window `[since_ms, until_ms]` (epoch-millis). Emits the coarsest summary whose whole subtree falls inside the window, and raw leaf chunks for anything not covered by such a summary (boundary content and not-yet-summarised chunks). Optional `source_id` / `source_kind` scope the result. Hits are grouped by source and ordered ascending by start time. Use this for time-bounded recaps (e.g. a last-24h morning brief) instead of `query_source`, which returns all-time summaries.

inputs.name: source_kind
inputs.comment: Source kind filter when no exact id is known.

outputs.name: hits
outputs.comment: Ordered list of hits (summaries and/or leaves).

methods.description: Purge one chunk plus its score row, entity-index rows, and on-disk .md file. Idempotent — missing chunk returns deleted=false. Does NOT cascade through sealed summaries; UIs warn the user.

outputs.name: deleted
outputs.comment: True when the chunk row was found and removed.

methods.description: Fully delete one document source by its EXACT source_id: every chunk plus its score / entity-index / embedding / reembed-skip side rows and chunk content files, the ingest dedup gates (bare source_id AND versioned source_id@version), and (when the source becomes fully orphaned) its source-scoped summary tree — summaries, summary embeddings + reembed-skip, tree entity-index, buffers, the tree row, and summary content files. Unlike delete_chunk this cascades, so stale summaries of the deleted source cannot resurface in recall, and it also finishes legacy partial deletes (chunks already gone, tree/gate left behind). Exact match only (never a prefix); shared collection/path_scope trees that summarise multiple documents are left intact. Idempotent — an unknown source_id returns deleted=false.

inputs.name: source_id
inputs.comment: Exact source id to remove (e.g. a Telegram note/event/meeting id).

outputs.name: deleted
outputs.comment: True when the call did real work: chunks were removed OR a stale orphaned source tree was cleaned (legacy case, chunks_removed=0).

outputs.name: chunks_removed
outputs.comment: Number of chunk rows removed for the source.

methods.description: One-shot Memory pipeline diagnostic (#002). Walks each stage (embeddings config, scheduler gate, job queue, extraction/recall degradation, summary-tree precondition) and returns per-stage health, the single first blocking cause (typed code + i18n remediation key), the degraded snapshot, and counters. Exposed for the agent's self-diagnosis and the CLI; cheap (config + queue counters + degraded flags, no live network probe).

outputs.name: healthy
outputs.comment: True when no stage is blocking (first_blocking_cause is null).

outputs.name: stages
outputs.comment: Ordered array of { stage, ok, failure?, note } — pipeline order, so the first non-ok stage is the first blocking cause.

outputs.name: first_blocking_cause
outputs.comment: Typed { code, class, remediation_key, detail? } of the first non-ok stage; null when healthy. Mirrors pipeline_status.first_blocking_cause as an explicit Option.

outputs.name: counters
outputs.comment: { total_chunks, jobs_ready, jobs_running, jobs_failed, extraction_coverage: number|null }. extraction_coverage is the fraction [0,1] of chunks with ≥1 indexed entity; null when the metric could not be measured (DB error).

methods.description: Walk a summary node's children one step (or more if `max_depth > 1`). Returns leaf chunks when the input is an L1 summary, or lower-level summaries when the input is L2+. When `query` is provided, children are reranked by cosine similarity to the query embedding — useful when a summary has many children and only the relevant ones are needed.

inputs.name: max_depth
inputs.comment: How many levels down to walk (default 1).

inputs.name: query
inputs.comment: Optional free-text query; when set, children are reranked by cosine similarity to the query embedding and unembedded children sort to the bottom.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.comment: Optional cap on returned hits, applied after rerank.

outputs.name: hits
outputs.comment: Hydrated child hits; empty on leaves or unknown ids.

methods.description: Aggregated Memory Tree health snapshot (#1856 Part 1). Returns a coarse `status` string (running/paused/syncing/error/idle), an optional human-readable reason, the most-recent chunk timestamp, the total chunk count, the on-disk wiki size in bytes, and per-state job counters from `mem_tree_jobs`. Polled by the Memory Tree status panel; cheap enough to call every couple of seconds.

outputs.name: status
outputs.comment: Coarse, UI-shaped status. Precedence: paused > error > degraded > syncing > running > idle. `degraded` (#002) = the pipeline runs but recall/structure is reduced.

outputs.name: reason
outputs.comment: Human-readable reason for the current status — present for `paused` (gate mode) and `error` (failed-job count).

outputs.name: last_sync_ms
outputs.comment: Epoch ms of the newest chunk timestamp across all sources; 0 when the store is empty.

outputs.name: wiki_size_bytes
outputs.comment: Recursive on-disk size of the `wiki/` sub-tree under the memory_tree content root. 0 when the directory does not exist yet.

outputs.name: is_syncing
outputs.comment: True when at least one job is in `running` state.

outputs.name: degraded
outputs.comment: #002 (FR-002/FR-004): object `{ semantic_recall: bool, structure: bool, cause?: PipelineFailure }`. The pipeline ran but output quality is reduced — `semantic_recall` when embeddings were skipped, `structure` when extraction yielded nothing. `cause` is the single precedence-resolved failure (structure over semantic_recall) and is OMITTED when no degradation is active; the recall/structure flags are tracked independently behind it. The object itself is always present (serde default). Distinct from a hard `error`.

outputs.name: first_blocking_cause
outputs.comment: #002 (FR-004): the single most-urgent typed cause as a `PipelineFailure` object `{ code, class, remediation_key }`. A failed job's classified reason wins over a soft degradation cause. null when healthy. The UI resolves `remediation_key` and renders it verbatim.

outputs.name: extraction_coverage
outputs.comment: #002 (FR-010): fraction [0.0, 1.0] of chunks with ≥1 indexed entity. Near 0 with total_chunks > 0 means extraction produces no structure. `null` when the metric could not be measured (DB read error) — deliberately distinct from a genuine `0.0` so a broken measurement is never misreported as a structure failure.

methods.description: Return summaries from one or more per-source trees. Filter by `source_id` (exact), `source_kind` (chat/email/document), and/or `time_window_days`. methods.description: Read a slice of a skill run's streaming log file by run_id. The FE Skills Runner panel opens this on click of a Recent Runs row and re-calls it every 2s while the run's `status` is RUNNING to tail new bytes (use the returned `offset` as the next call's `offset`). The run id resolves to a path internally — callers don't supply a path, so no traversal surface. `max_bytes` is clamped to 262144 (256 KiB) per call; pages by re-issuing with the returned `offset`.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.comment: Run id from `skills_recent_runs.runs[].run_id` (matched by 8-char prefix against the log filename).

inputs.name: offset
inputs.comment: Byte offset to start reading from. Default 0 (read from start); the FE passes the previous response's `offset` for tail-mode polling.

inputs.name: max_bytes
inputs.comment: Max bytes to return in this slice. Default 65536 (64 KiB), capped at 262144 (256 KiB).

outputs.name: offset
outputs.comment: New read cursor — pass this as the next call's `offset` to tail forward.

outputs.name: bytes_read
outputs.comment: Number of bytes returned in this slice.

outputs.name: content
outputs.comment: The slice contents (UTF-8, lossy-decoded so a partial multibyte tail doesn't error).

outputs.name: eof
outputs.comment: True if the read reached end-of-file. May still be FALSE-complete (run still streaming).

outputs.name: complete
outputs.comment: True once the run footer (`--- result ---`) has landed in the file. The FE stops polling when this flips true.

methods.description: List recent autonomous skill runs by scanning `<workspace>/skills/.runs/`. Returns one entry per log file (header: workflow_id, run_id, started; footer: status, duration_ms, finished) sorted by `started` descending. `status` is `RUNNING` while the footer hasn't landed yet, then `DONE` / `DEGENERATE` / `FAILED`. Optionally filter by `workflow_id` to scope to one skill; `limit` (default 20, max 100) caps the result. Cheap: reads the files top-to-bottom and short-circuits — no schema parsing of the streaming body.

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.comment: Optional: restrict results to runs of one skill (e.g. "github-issue-crusher"). Omit to return runs across every skill.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.comment: Cap on the number of entries returned. Default 20, clamped to 100.

outputs.name: runs
outputs.comment: Array of `{ run_id, workflow_id, started, status, duration_ms, finished, log_path }` — see crate::openhuman::skills::run_log::ScannedRun.
